/thinking/engineering-philosophy.html.description: Four principles for choosing engineering interventions: start with the bottleneck, systematize repetition, preserve judgment, and keep evidence close to claims.

/thinking/why-does-this-still-require-me.description: A practical engineering heuristic for recognizing repeated work that should become a system, delegated responsibility, or automated workflow.

/thinking/release-reports-as-operational.description: How comparable release-health reports can reveal longer-term engineering patterns while AI assistance remains accountable to human judgment.

/thinking/blockchain-without-a-master-br.title: Blockchain Without a Master Branch | Mantosh Kumar
/thinking/blockchain-without-a-master-br.description: A limited Git analogy for understanding blockchain as a distributed shared-history problem without a central source of truth.

/thinking/ownership-before-escalation.ht.title: Engineering Ownership Before Escalation | Mantosh Kumar
/thinking/ownership-before-escalation.ht.description: A practical communication standard for making material engineering issues, risks, and next actions visible before someone has to ask.
